name: Goan Fish Curry
tagline: Coconut + tamarind fish curry — omega-3, vitamin D, B12, selenium in one bowl

hero.alt: Orange-red fish curry with chunks of pomfret simmering in coconut tamarind gravy
hero.caption: Goan fish curry — coconut + tamarind

steps.title: Marinate the fish
steps.detail: Rub fish pieces with ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp salt, and 1 tsp lime juice. Set aside 15 minutes — firms the flesh and seasons through.
steps.tip: Don't skip — unseasoned fish in a strong gravy tastes hollow.

steps.title: Grind and sauté the masala
steps.detail: Soak Kashmiri chilies in hot water 5 minutes. Grind with coconut, coriander, cumin, tamarind, and ½ cup water to a smooth paste. Heat coconut oil, sauté onion till translucent, add ginger-garlic, then the masala paste. Cook 6 minutes till oil separates.
steps.tip: Cook the paste — raw masala = bitter curry.

steps.title: Simmer the fish
steps.detail: Pour in 1.5 cups water, bring to a gentle simmer, slide in the fish pieces in a single layer. Cook 8 minutes — never stir vigorously, just shake the pan. Adjust salt and tang.
steps.tip: Fish is done when it flakes at the touch of a spoon — overcooked fish goes rubbery.

healthFacts: Fatty fish provides EPA + DHA omega-3s — directly raises the omega-3 index.
Coconut milk's MCTs raise HDL more than they raise LDL.
Tamarind's polyphenols add antioxidant load and a digestion-friendly tang.
